column11: Where the Gulf meets the Grand Mosque in white marble and turquoise.

column15: Shallow turquoise water, white sand, and a skyline built to be seen. The Grand Mosque visible from a hotel terrace at night, the Al Bateen marina after dark, Saadiyat's Gulf horizon — and the Zayed National Museum and the Yas Island parks close enough to reshape the itinerary without leaving the city behind. No wonder Abu Dhabi keeps drawing us back.

column11: Pastel villages perched above the Tyrrhenian, Positano to Vietri sul Mare.

column14: From Le Sirenuse's candlelit terrace to Caruso's infinity pool at 365m, the Amalfi Coast's 18 best-view hotels — with room recommendations for every property.
column15: The Amalfi Coast runs for 50 kilometres between Positano and Vietri sul Mare, and every hotel on this list was chosen for a single reason: the view from the room. We've covered the full range — cliff-perched monasteries above the Gulf of Salerno, family-run four-stars in Praiano, and the five-star properties in Positano and Ravello that have defined the coast's reputation since the 1950s.

column11: Canal reflections at dusk where every gabled house holds the light differently.

column15: Amsterdam's views are canal views — but not all of them work the same way. The Herengracht, Prinsengracht, and Keizersgracht run through the historic core; the Amstel opens to the east; the IJ connects the city to the sea. What Amsterdam asks of a hotel is straightforward: put the water in the window, and these hotels surely rise to the expectations.

column11: The Taurus mountains meeting the Mediterranean from cliff-top hotel terraces.

column11: The Acropolis lit at night above three thousand years of skyline.

column15: Athens has one view: the Acropolis, and in this city, it follows you — from the room window to the rooftop pool to the breakfast table. These hotels were chosen for exactly that — from the base of the rock in Monastiraki to the wider angle above Syntagma, and the Syngrou corridor where it sits between Lycabettus and the sea.

column11: Two harbours, fifty volcanoes, and rooftop views over the Hauraki Gulf.

column14: The Viaduct marina, Wynyard Quarter waterfront, and CBD suites above the roofline — 17 Auckland hotels selected for the view, with room picks for each.
column15: Auckland sits on an isthmus between two harbours, and its hotels reflect that geography: some face the Waitematā from the waterfront, others catch the Sky Tower from CBD towers above the surrounding roofline, and a few manage both at once. Seventeen properties are featured here, from a hotel built over a wharf on Princes Wharf to a Britomart rooftop with the harbour to the north and the city behind it.

column11: Rice terraces, volcanic ridges, and ocean cliffs that defined the infinity pool.

Every private villa and pool here was chosen for one reason: the view alone is worth the trip.

Above the river corridor, the city skyline rises high enough for open-air bars above the 60th floor and suites with the full Bangkok panorama below.

The best hotels in this guide earn their position by putting the Sagrada Família above the roofline, the Cathedral of Barcelona at eye level, or the sea visible from the room — not from the lobby. From the Eixample to Barceloneta, from the Gothic Quarter to the Tibidabo ridge, every hotel below was chosen for what you'll actually see.

column14: The Forbidden City's rooftops, Beijing's CCTV Headquarters at room level, and the CBD skyline — eight of the best hotels in the Chinese capital, with room picks for each.
column15: From a room in Beijing, the view is always a choice between the ancient and the modern — imperial rooflines that have stood for six centuries, or a skyline that turns checking in into something worth remembering.

column11: Spree-facing balconies and Reichstag rooftops over a city rewritten every decade.

column11: Brick townhouses and harbour light, from Beacon Hill to the Atlantic.

column15: Boston's most compelling views come from the water's edge — or from across it. The harbor defines the city's eastern skyline, and the hotels on this list sit either on it, above it, or facing it from the East Boston shore. Back Bay adds a second axis: the Prudential tower, Fenway's lights to the west, and the kind of unobstructed height that only comes from building on landfill.

column11: Pebble beach, pier silhouette, and the English Channel at every window.

column15: Brighton's seafront runs along a single promenade, which makes the hotel choice unusually direct: the view either faces the English Channel or it doesn't. The properties here were selected for confirmed sightlines — from the Victorian grandeur of Kings Road to the Regency townhouses of Kemptown and the enclosed basin of Brighton Marina.

column11: The Story Bridge mirrored in the river at every tide change.
column13: Brisbane Hotels with the Best Views of the River & Story Bridge — The Most Perfect View
column14: Story Bridge from a rooftop bar, the river from a 74th-floor suite, South Bank from a CBD balcony — curated hotels with room recommendations.

The view might come from a 21st-floor rooftop terrace, a riverfront pool deck directly beneath a bridge, or a suite on the 74th floor looking across the full sweep of the city.

column11: Buda Castle and Parliament glowing across the Danube at every blue hour.

The Chain Bridge, the Basilica, the castle district at night — and rooftop bars that understand what it means to have a view worth staying up for. Budapest rewards the choice of which bank to book.

column11: Belle Époque rooftops and the Río de la Plata in soft Patagonian light.

column11: Pyramids on the horizon and the Nile threading through six thousand years.

The views here are specific — from a room on Princes Street looking west, from a high floor above the Royal Mile, or from a rooftop above the New Town — and the hotel that captures each one best is rarely the obvious choice. These nine properties cover the castle, the city skyline, the Old Town spires, and Arthur's Seat.

Whether the view is in the room or above it depends on where you book.

column14: Three Rocks Beach below the Ritz-Carlton, Pillar Point Harbor from boutique suites, Mavericks in the distance. Four hotels selected for view quality — with room picks for each.

The hotels here divide across two positions: the southern bluffs above Three Rocks Beach, where the Ritz-Carlton commands the cliff edge, and Pillar Point Harbor to the north, where fishing boats and whale-watching tours leave from the same dock.

column11: Elbphilharmonie above the harbour — Germany's most cinematic port city.

column11: Victoria Harbour skyline, the Peak, and three sides of South China Sea.

The hotels on this list are positioned directly on that view, across Tsim Sha Tsui, West Kowloon, Central, Admiralty, and Wan Chai, each verified for view substance rather than proximity alone. We've included the full range: waterfront rooms at eye level, upper-floor suites above the harbour, and the outliers that earn their place for different reasons.

column14: Balconies above the surf, rooftop infinity pools facing Diamond Head, and private-beach retreats beyond the crater. Curated for the view — with room picks for each property.

column11: Hagia Sophia, the Bosphorus, and two continents from a single window.

column11: Petronas Towers above rainforest canopy, still visible from rooftop pools.

The hotels on this list are chosen for how close that view gets: from rooms in the KLCC corridor where the towers fill the window, to the floors of Merdeka 118 where they appear below the horizon.

column11: Coves, cliffs, and kelp gardens along Southern California's most painted coast.

column15: Laguna Beach runs for seven miles of cliffside coves and rocky headlands between Newport Beach and Dana Point. The hotels here range from blufftop resorts above private stretches of sand to canyon retreats a short walk from the surf.

column11: Cypress-lined villas and pre-Alpine peaks reflected in mirror-still water.
column13: Lake Como Hotels with Views of Bellagio, the Alps & the Lake — The Most Perfect View
column14: Balconies over the lake in Bellagio and Tremezzo, floating pools on the water, a Michelin terrace at dusk. Room recommendations for each of the 11 hotels.

The view belongs to a room, a floating pool, or a terrace, depending on the hotel — and in several cases, to all three.

column11: England's lakes, fells, and Wordsworth country in shifting Cumbrian light.

These nine hotels span direct waterfront positions, hilltop vantages above the lake, and remote fell retreats deep in Borrowdale and the central fells.

column11: Italy's largest lake — Dolomites to the north, lemon groves to the south.

The northern basin near Riva del Garda frames limestone mountains on both sides; further south, the lake opens into a broader panorama. The hotels on this list sit on both shores — with views that belong to a room, a terrace, or an infinity pool, depending on where you stay.

column11: The Strip from a high floor, the Mojave Desert in every direction.

column11: Seven hills of tiled façades and the Tagus catching every kind of light.

column14: Castle-hill sightlines, Tagus riverfront suites, and Alfama rooftops — 22 Lisbon hotels chosen for what you'll actually see from the room. Room recommendations for each.
column15: Older than almost any European capital and built on seven hills above the Tagus, Lisbon stacks its best hotel views between two axes: the river and the castle. The 22 hotels below range from the waterfront in Belém to the Alfama slopes directly below São Jorge Castle.

column14: 13 Seville hotels chosen for where they actually put you — opposite La Giralda, facing the Cathedral, or above Plaza de España. Room picks for every property
column15: Seville concentrates its most significant landmarks — the Cathedral, La Giralda, and the Royal Alcázar — in a compact historic centre that makes choosing a hotel a meaningful decision. The properties on this list were selected for the quality and proximity of their views: rooftop pools that face the Cathedral directly, suite terraces with the Giralda in frame, and one hotel that breaks from that axis entirely to face Plaza de España.

column11: The Bund's colonial façades facing Pudong's lit skyline across the Huangpu.
column13: Shanghai Hotels with Views of The Bund and Pudong Skyline — The Most Perfect View
column14: The Oriental Pearl Tower and Jin Mao Tower visible from Bund-side rooms, the historic waterfront from Pudong. The best Shanghai hotels for views — 11 picks, room recommendations.
column15: Shanghai splits its greatest views across two banks. From The Bund, the Pudong skyline — the Pearl Tower, Jin Mao Tower, and Shanghai Tower — stands little more than a kilometre across the Huangpu River; from Pudong, the historic colonial facades of The Bund face back across the water. The hotels on this page sit on both sides of that exchange, ranging from century-old waterfront landmarks to the highest hotel rooms in China.

column11: South China's youngest skyline — glass towers above the Pearl River Delta.
column13: Shenzhen Hotels with Views of the Skyline, Shenzhen Bay & Lotus Hill — The Most Perfect View
column14: From Futian towers to Nanshan's coastal heights — hotels selected for confirmed views of Lianhuashan, Shenzhen Bay, and the skyline. Room picks for each.
column15: Shenzhen puts its best hotels at altitude — the towers of Futian and Nanshan begin above the 60th floor, not below. The properties here, from supertalls in the central business district to a new coastal tower above Shenzhen Bay, were chosen because the view from the room is the reason to be there.

column11: Marina Bay infinity pools, supertree groves, the Strait of Singapore at horizon.
column13: Singapore Hotels with Views of Marina Bay Sands & the Skyline — The Most Perfect View
column14: Singapore hotels selected for genuine Marina Bay and skyline views — rooftop pools, bay-facing suites, and room picks for every property.
column15: Singapore frames its waterfront with an unusual consistency: the hotels closest to Marina Bay have views that are structured, not incidental — Marina Bay Sands from one side, the Esplanade from another, Gardens by the Bay below. This selection covers the full range of the bay, from hotels built directly over the water to rooftop bars at altitude, each chosen because the view is the strongest reason to book.

column11: Palaces hidden in Atlantic mist — Portugal's most romantic forested ridge.
column13: Sintra Hotels with Views of Pena Palace, the Moorish Castle & the Atlantic — The Most Perfect View
column14: 8 Sintra hotels chosen for what you'll actually see — the red battlements of the Moorish Castle, Pena Palace on the ridge, and Cape Roca over the Atlantic. Room recommendations for each.
column15: Sintra's ridge holds the Moorish Castle and Pena Palace above a forested mountain chain that ends at the Atlantic. The hotels here were chosen for a view from one of three positions — the ridge, the village, or the coast.

column11: An archipelago capital of waterfront views across fourteen Nordic-lit islands.
column13: Best Hotels in Stockholm with Royal Palace & Waterfront Views — The Most Perfect View
column14: Top Stockholm waterfront hotels chosen for the view from the room — with specific room picks, from Nybroviken bay to the Gamla Stan Old Town waterfront.
column15: Few capitals place their best hotels as deliberately on the water as Stockholm does. From the Royal Palace waterfront to Saltsjö Bay, the ten properties here were selected for one criterion: the quality of what you see when you stay.

column11: Opera House sails and Harbour Bridge — the most photographed waterfront on earth.
column13: Sydney Hotels with Views of the Harbour Bridge and Opera House — The Most Perfect View
column14: Sydney hotels selected for harbour, skyline, and beach views — the Harbour Bridge, Opera House, Bondi, and Coogee. Room recommendations for every property.
column15: Sydney's most photographed views extend well beyond the harbour — from the Harbour Bridge and Opera House at Circular Quay to the Pacific at Coogee Beach and the skyline reading from both shores. We've selected properties for the quality of what's in the window, on every side of the city.

column11: Tokyo Tower, Mount Fuji on clear winter mornings, neon stretching to every horizon.
column13: Tokyo Hotels with Views of Tokyo Tower, Fuji & the Palace — The Most Perfect View
column14: 24 hotels selected for what you'll actually see — the red-and-white lattice at close range, the Imperial Palace gardens, Rainbow Bridge, and Mount Fuji on clear mornings. Room recommendations for each.
column15: Tokyo's best hotel views divide cleanly by subject: Tokyo Tower close enough to fill the window, the Imperial Palace gardens spread below a breakfast table, Rainbow Bridge and the bay from an Odaiba waterfront room, or Mount Fuji at the horizon on a clear winter morning from Shinjuku. The 24 hotels below were selected because the view is the point — not incidental to the room, but the reason for it.

column11: CN Tower above Lake Ontario, a glass skyline reflected at every lakeshore window.
column13: Toronto Hotels with Views of the CN Tower and Lake Ontario — The Most Perfect View
column14: A 36-suite boutique above the 41st floor, a 1929 landmark opposite Union Station — 10 hotels chosen for confirmed sightlines of the tower, the lake, or both.
column15: The CN Tower and Lake Ontario are Toronto's two dominant views — one vertical, one horizontal, often closer than expected. The hotels here put you in front of one or both.

column11: North Shore mountains, Stanley Park seawall, and Pacific inlets framing the city.
column13: Vancouver Hotels with the Best Waterfront and Mountain Views — The Most Perfect View
column14: Hotels in Vancouver selected for Coal Harbour, Stanley Park, and North Shore Mountain views. Specific room recommendations for every property.
column15: Vancouver's Coal Harbour places mountains, inlet, and the edge of Stanley Park into one frame. The hotels here run from waterfront suites directly above the marina to towers overlooking False Creek and the downtown skyline.

column11: Where centuries of palaces rise straight from the lagoon at every tide.
column13: Venice Hotels with Views of the Grand Canal & Rialto Bridge — The Most Perfect View
column14: Canal rooms and romantic balconies at water level, the bridge at close range, San Giorgio Maggiore across the lagoon. Fifteen hotels with view picks for each.
column15: The Grand Canal is Venice's main artery and its most romantic view — from palazzo windows, canal-side terraces, and dining rooms that hover just above the water. The Rialto Bridge, the dome of Santa Maria della Salute, and San Giorgio Maggiore across the basin keep reappearing from different angles throughout the city. St. Mark's Square anchors the eastern end of it all, and the hotels closest to it tend to have the most to show.

column11: Hofburg rooftops, the Danube curve, and Vienna Woods on the western horizon.
column13: Vienna Hotels with Views of St. Stephen's Cathedral & the Opera — The Most Perfect View
column14: Cathedral suite balconies at Stephansplatz, the Opera façade from Kärntner Ring, the Ringstrasse from a rooftop pool — 15 properties with room recommendations.
column15: Vienna's most photographed view depends on which side of the city you're standing. From the Innere Stadt, the view looks up at St. Stephen's Cathedral's diamond-tiled spire; from the Ringstrasse hotels, it's the State Opera and the tree-lined boulevard that fill the frame. The properties below range from Petersplatz in the historic core to the Danube skyline in Donaustadt — all chosen because the view is the argument.

column11: Three miles of boardwalk where the Atlantic meets the Chesapeake Bay's mouth.
column13: Virginia Beach Hotels with Oceanfront Views — The Most Perfect View
column14: The boardwalk below, the Atlantic from a private balcony, Orion's Roof 23 floors up. Fourteen hotels, budget to luxury, with room recommendations.
column15: Virginia Beach's oceanfront runs three unbroken miles along the Atlantic, with every hotel on this list placed directly between the boardwalk and the water. The range here goes from a 1927 landmark with a private beach club to newly opened all-suite towers — all chosen because the view holds, regardless of floor or category.

column11: Monuments along the Potomac, the Mall in every season's changing light.
column13: Washington DC Hotels with White House and Monument Views — The Most Perfect View
column14: Waterfront suites on the Tidal Basin, rooftops overlooking the White House, Capitol-view rooms steps from the Hill — independently reviewed.
column15: Washington is a city built to be looked at — the Capitol at the end of the Mall, the Washington Monument rising above the floodplains, the White House behind its wrought-iron fence. Here, the view from a hotel window is often the same view that defines the city itself. We've chosen properties where the National Mall, the Potomac River, and the city's defining landmarks are part of the stay — not just visible from the outside.

column11: Lake reflections and Alps on clear horizons — Limmat-side rooftops in alpine light.
column13: Zurich Hotels with Views of Lake Zurich, Old Town & the Alps — The Most Perfect View
column14: Lakefront rooms on Utoquai, hillside suites above the fog line, a rooftop thermal pool over the water — the Zurich hotels where Lake Zurich is the view.
column15: Lake Zurich is the view that defines the city — but not every hotel that claims it actually delivers. The lakefront rooms on Utoquai, the hilltop suites that look down over the water from above the fog, and the summit hotel at 871 metres where the lake fills the window at dawn: these are the positions worth knowing before you book.

column11: Aegean coves, the Castle of St Peter, and Turkey's most photographed Mediterranean coast.
column13: Bodrum Hotels with the Best Views
column14: The Castle of St. Peter as a view object, Kos Island offshore, Yalikavak Marina as a foreground — six properties selected where the Aegean view is the point.
column15: More layered than the Aegean coast to its south, Bodrum stacks its views: a 15th-century castle above the harbor, the Greek island of Kos offshore, and a marina that sets the foreground for some of the properties below.
